title: Google Analytics / gtag loaded before consent
description: Detects Google Analytics or Google Tag Manager scripts loaded directly in HTML without consent gating. Under GDPR and ePrivacy Directive, analytics scripts require prior user consent before activation.
remediation: Set script type to 'text/plain' and add a data-consent attribute. Only change the type to 'text/javascript' after the user grants consent via your CMP. Alternatively, use Google Consent Mode v2 with default denied state.

title: Meta / Facebook Pixel loaded without consent gate
description: Detects the Meta (Facebook) tracking pixel loaded in page markup without consent verification. The pixel sets third-party cookies and transmits browsing data to Meta servers, requiring explicit user consent under GDPR.
remediation: Wrap the Facebook Pixel in a consent-gated loader. Do not include the script tag directly in HTML. Load it dynamically only after the user accepts marketing cookies via your consent management platform.

title: document.cookie set without consent check
description: Detects direct assignment to document.cookie without any consent verification in the surrounding code. Non-essential cookies must not be set until the user has actively consented.
remediation: Implement a consent manager and check consent status before setting any non-essential cookie. Use a cookie abstraction layer that enforces consent checks: consentManager.setCookie('analytics', name, value).

title: Tracking SDK initialized at page load without consent
description: Detects third-party tracking SDKs (Hotjar, Mixpanel, Amplitude, PostHog, Heap, FullStory, LogRocket) initialized at page load without consent verification. These SDKs collect behavioral data and must only be activated after user consent.
remediation: Move SDK initialization behind a consent gate. Initialize the SDK only after the user grants consent for the relevant cookie category. Provide a wrapper function that queues events until consent is obtained.
